text:
User: when an agent creates a channel it should auto switch to it [...] **1. Auto-switch into a channel on create**
After `channels create` provisions the new room, the daemon now re-homes the creating agent's session into it — the same route-scope + identity move that `channels switch` performs. I extracted that logic into a shared `rehome_session_to_channel` helper so create and switch can't drift apart. The brand-new room needs none of the switch path's occupancy/membership guards (the creator is its only member and no other session can already sign for it there), so the auto-switch is unconditional for genuine agent sessions. The CLI prints `switched to it`. [...] and --agent should not be required [...] **2. `--agent` is no longer required**
Dropped the "at least one `--agent`" bail in both the CLI and the daemon. With no targets, the channel is created and joined but **no** kind:9 orchestration event is published (an add-agents event with zero `add` tags is meaningless). `orchestration_event_id` comes back empty in that case. [...] One deliberate choice worth flagging: a `create` that hits the dedup path (name already exists) does **not** auto-switch — switching into a pre-existing channel needs the occupancy checks that only `channels switch` runs. Tell me if you'd rather have dedup route through the full switch instead.
